name: Santa Cruz
h1: General Contractor in Santa Cruz, CA
metaTitle: Santa Cruz General Contractor | Remodels & ADUs | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Licensed general contractor serving the City of Santa Cruz — kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, additions, and whole-home renovations. CSLB #1075110. Free estimates.
lede: Gravity Construction is a locally owned, licensed general contractor working throughout the City of Santa Cruz — from the Westside and Seabright to Pleasure Point, the Upper Westside, and the downtown neighborhoods around Mission Hill.
bodyHtml:
Remodeling in Santa Cruz takes a contractor who knows the city

Santa Cruz homes are a patchwork — 1910 Craftsman bungalows on the Westside, 1940s beach cottages near Twin Lakes, post-war ranches off Soquel, and modern infill from the last decade. Each era brings its own surprises once the drywall comes off: knob-and-tube wiring, galvanized supply lines, unpermitted additions stacked by three previous owners, redwood sheathing that's beautiful but dry as a bone. We've opened up walls in every one of these vintages and know what to expect.

The City of Santa Cruz also has its own Building & Safety process, its own historic review in certain overlay zones, and a Coastal Commission zone that covers everything south of Mission Street down to the water. Permits in Santa Cruz move faster when the drawings anticipate city staff's questions — we prepare submittals with that in mind so your project doesn't get stuck in corrections cycles.

Neighborhoods we work in

  • Westside — Craftsman remodels, Title 24 upgrades, rear-yard ADUs. Tight lots mean site logistics matter; we stage small and keep neighbors in the loop.
  • Seabright & Eastside — Beach-cottage kitchen and bath remodels, foundation work on homes built before modern footing codes, second-story additions with ocean views.
  • Pleasure Point & 41st Avenue — Salt-air rated finishes, outdoor living upgrades, whole-home remodels on homes bought as surf rentals turned primary residences.
  • Upper Westside & UCSC-adjacent — Hillside additions, ADUs for multigenerational living, detached garage conversions.
  • Mission Hill & Downtown — Historic-sensitive kitchen remodels, seismic retrofits, deferred-maintenance whole-home projects on homes 100+ years old.
  • Harbor & Live Oak edge — Waterfront moisture management, custom built-ins, boat-storage-aware garage conversions.

Permits and inspections in the City of Santa Cruz

The City's Planning & Community Development Department runs permit submittals through an online portal (EnerGov). For kitchen and bath remodels, expect mechanical, electrical, and plumbing inspections plus a final. For ADUs, the state ministerial track (SB 9 / ADU streamlining) generally applies — 60-day review once the application is complete. Coastal zone projects add a Coastal Development Permit review, which is the single biggest schedule variable we see on Westside jobs.

We pull all permits in the owner's name or ours depending on what makes sense for your lender and insurance. Either way, you get copies of every approved drawing set and every inspection card.

What we build most often in the city

Kitchen remodels are the number-one request — usually opening up the wall between the kitchen and dining room, replacing original tile counters with quartz or soapstone, and upgrading the electrical panel since the existing one is almost always at capacity. Bath remodels run a close second; waterproofing is where we don't cut corners, because Santa Cruz humidity punishes sloppy pan details within a year.

ADUs are booming. The state now allows up to 1,200 sq ft detached and the city's fee schedule is predictable. Most of our ADU clients are building for aging parents, adult kids, or long-term rental income.

How to get started

Call (831) 234-4669 or use the contact form. We'll schedule a walk-through — usually within the week — and follow up with a written scope and budget range before design starts. No fee for the first visit.

faq.q: Do you work in the Coastal Zone?
faq.a: Yes. We've completed projects throughout the Santa Cruz Coastal Zone and are familiar with the Coastal Development Permit process. Expect an additional 60–120 days on the schedule when a CDP is required; we build that into the timeline upfront.

faq.q: How long does a kitchen remodel take in Santa Cruz?
faq.a: A typical mid-range kitchen remodel runs 8–12 weeks on site, plus 4–6 weeks of design and permitting. Historic-district homes or projects with structural changes can push to 14–16 weeks.

faq.q: Can you build an ADU on my Westside lot?
faq.a: In most cases yes. California state law preempts a lot of local restrictions — setbacks as tight as 4 feet, no off-street parking required near transit, and ministerial approval. We'll check your specific APN for overlay zones before committing.

name: Capitola
metaTitle: Capitola General Contractor | Remodels & Coastal Builds | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Licensed contractor for Capitola Village, Jewel Box, and Depot Hill. Kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, and coastal-rated whole-home renovations. CSLB #1075110.
lede: Capitola's tight lots, historic charm, and literal oceanfront exposure make it one of the most rewarding — and least forgiving — places we build. We've done everything from Jewel Box cottage refreshes to Depot Hill whole-home remodels.
bodyHtml:
Building in a village where every inch counts

Capitola Village is platted on a grid that dates to 1869. Lots are narrow, setbacks are short, and many of the original cottages sit on rubble foundations or un-reinforced post-and-pier. When we take on a Capitola remodel, the first thing we do is a structural walk-through — because you can't bolt modern cabinetry and tile to a foundation that's six inches off plumb without fixing the underlying issue.

The City of Capitola has its own planning counter, its own design review in the Village overlay, and shares Coastal Commission jurisdiction with the county. Historic preservation matters here; the wrong window shape on a Village-zoned cottage can kill a permit faster than any other single factor.

Neighborhoods we work in

  • Capitola Village — Beach cottages with heavy salt exposure, narrow-lot kitchen remodels, exterior work that has to pass Village design review.
  • Jewel Box — Pastel-painted cottages that owners want to modernize inside without losing the curb appeal. Interior-only remodels are our sweet spot here.
  • Depot Hill — Ocean-view homes with the harshest wind-and-salt exposure in the county. Finish selection matters; we steer clients toward marine-grade hardware and thermally broken window systems.
  • 41st Avenue corridor — Mid-century tract homes getting their first or second major remodel. Electrical and plumbing are always in the scope.
  • New Brighton & McGregor Drive — Larger lots, more freedom on additions and ADUs, but hillside grading rules apply.

Coastal exposure is not a talking point — it's a material spec

In Capitola we default to stainless 316 fasteners, marine-grade plywood substrates in wet areas, fiber-cement or composite siding rather than wood, and fiberglass or aluminum-clad windows over vinyl. These upgrades add 5–10% to a finish budget but pay back in decades of not having to redo the work. Clients who come to us after a cheaper contractor used galvanized hardware usually regret the savings within three years.

Permits in Capitola

The city processes permits through its own small Building Department; response times are generally 3–6 weeks for a standard remodel and 8–12 weeks for Village-overlay projects that need design review. We handle the submittal and the back-and-forth with staff so you're not dropping by City Hall during your lunch break.

Recent projects in Capitola

See the Projects section for completed work, or call (831) 234-4669 to walk through your house.

faq.q: Can I add a second story to my Capitola Village cottage?
faq.a: Sometimes — it depends on your lot's zoning and whether the structure can support it. Many Village cottages are best as one-story remodels with rear additions. We'll pull zoning and do a structural check before promising anything.

faq.q: What does salt-air exposure actually cost in a remodel?
faq.a: Roughly 5–10% more on finishes and hardware, depending on how close you are to the water. The alternative is re-doing corroded fasteners and rusted anchors within 3–5 years.

name: Aptos
metaTitle: Aptos General Contractor | Whole-Home & Kitchen Remodels | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Aptos remodel contractor — Rio del Mar, Seacliff, Aptos Hills. Kitchens, baths, additions, ADUs, and whole-home renovations with coastal-rated specs. CSLB #1075110.
lede: From Rio del Mar oceanfronts to Aptos Hills ranches, we build and remodel across every corner of Aptos. Our clients here tend to be long-term owners making once-in-a-generation investments in their homes.
bodyHtml:
Aptos is where Santa Cruz County gets serious about remodels

The median Aptos home has more square footage, more yard, and more deferred maintenance than its city-of-Santa-Cruz counterpart. Our Aptos clients usually aren't refreshing a single room — they're doing whole-home remodels, second-story additions, ADUs on the back of the lot, and kitchen-plus-primary-suite combos that take 4–6 months on site.

Aptos is unincorporated Santa Cruz County, so permits go through County Planning (701 Ocean Street) rather than a city. The county's ministerial ADU track is efficient; discretionary work like hillside additions takes longer because Geotechnical Review gets involved.

Neighborhoods we work in

  • Rio del Mar flats — Beach-adjacent tracts, 1960s–1980s construction, usually needing full electrical and plumbing upgrades.
  • Rio del Mar bluffs — Ocean-view homes where finish spec really matters; we've built several rear decks and outdoor kitchens here.
  • Seacliff — Historic cottages near Seacliff State Beach, narrow access, careful staging required.
  • Aptos Village & Trout Gulch — Remodels of homes that predate the Village redevelopment; character-rich but wiring-challenged.
  • Aptos Hills & Larsen Road — Larger properties, occasional septic and well work in coordination with our trade partners, barn-style ADUs and workshops.
  • Day Valley & Freedom Boulevard — Semi-rural parcels, agricultural overlays, ADU detached builds over 1,000 sq ft.

Whole-home remodels are our bread and butter in Aptos

A typical Aptos whole-home runs $400K–$900K with a 6–9 month timeline. We'll usually recommend doing electrical, plumbing, insulation, windows, HVAC, and the kitchen in one mobilization rather than spreading it across phases — it's 20–30% cheaper overall and the house comes back together as a cohesive system rather than a series of patches.

Hillside work and geotech

If your Aptos property has any slope — and most do — expect a geotechnical report in the permit package for additions or foundation work. We coordinate with local soils engineers we've used for years; they know the county's expectations and don't over-spec.

Ready to start?

Call (831) 234-4669 or send us a message. First meeting is on us.

faq.q: Do you do whole-home remodels while we live in the house?
faq.a: For remodels under about 40% of the home we can usually phase around you. For larger scopes we strongly recommend relocating — it saves money, shortens the schedule, and preserves your sanity.

faq.q: How does the county ADU process work in Aptos?
faq.a: Submit to County Planning, get a 60-day ministerial review for standard ADUs under 1,200 sq ft, then building permit and construction. Total from contract to move-in is typically 8–12 months.

name: Soquel
metaTitle: Soquel General Contractor | Kitchen, Bath & ADU Remodels | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Soquel remodel contractor — Soquel Village, Soquel Drive, Old San Jose Road. Kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, whole-home renovations. CSLB #1075110.
lede: Soquel sits at the crossroads of Santa Cruz County — close enough to the coast to feel the breeze, far enough inland to escape the fog. We remodel across the entire 95073 zip, from the Village to the top of Old San Jose Road.
bodyHtml:
Soquel's mix of housing stock keeps the work interesting

You'll find 1970s ranches, 1920s Craftsmans in the Village, 1990s tract homes off Soquel Drive, and multi-acre Old San Jose Road properties all within a three-mile radius. Our Soquel projects run the full range: $80K kitchen refreshes, $350K ADUs, $600K whole-home remodels on Porter Gulch.

Soquel is unincorporated — permits go through Santa Cruz County. The permit experience is the same as Aptos and Live Oak: file through the County portal, expect a standard remodel to clear plan check in 4–8 weeks, longer if any hillside or riparian issues are involved.

Areas we cover in Soquel

  • Soquel Village — Historic core, character homes with older systems, careful about neighborhood compatibility.
  • Soquel Drive corridor — Mid-century to late-20th-century stock, often first-time major remodels.
  • Old San Jose Road — Semi-rural, larger lots, well and septic work coordinated with our subs.
  • Porter Gulch & Cherryvale — Hillside parcels with geotech considerations and longer driveways (staging matters).
  • Mar Vista & 41st Avenue edge — Tract homes sharing characteristics with Capitola; kitchen and bath remodels are frequent.

Common Soquel projects

Kitchen remodels where the cook wants an island but the existing layout is a galley. Primary suite additions on homes that were built with tiny bedrooms by modern standards. Detached ADUs built as home offices or for renting — the Soquel rental market is strong and stable. Workshop-garage builds for the many Soquel residents who run trade businesses out of their homes.

Start the conversation

Call (831) 234-4669. We respond to calls within one business day.

faq.q: Can you work on a rural Soquel property with a well and septic?
faq.a: Yes. We coordinate with licensed well and septic contractors and pull Environmental Health permits when the project adds bedrooms or plumbing load. Septic capacity is often the limiting factor on ADU builds, so we check it early.

name: Live Oak
h1: General Contractor in Live Oak, CA
metaTitle: Live Oak General Contractor | Remodels & ADUs | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Live Oak remodel contractor — Pleasure Point, East Cliff, 7th Avenue. Kitchens, baths, ADUs, additions. Coastal-rated construction. CSLB #1075110.
lede: Live Oak runs from the Santa Cruz city limit at Rodriguez Street down past Pleasure Point to the Capitola line. It's the densest unincorporated area in the county and the one where we see the most variety in our Monday schedule.
bodyHtml:
Live Oak is where Santa Cruz meets beach town

Homes near Pleasure Point were built as weekend cottages in the 1940s and 1950s and are now year-round residences getting their first or second serious remodel. Lots are small, street parking is short, and surfers walking by at 6 AM is a scheduling consideration we actually factor in when planning deliveries.

Live Oak is unincorporated, so permits go through Santa Cruz County. Most of the Live Oak south of East Cliff Drive falls within the Coastal Zone — expect a CDP review for any exterior work, which adds 2–4 months to the permit timeline.

Areas we cover in Live Oak

  • Pleasure Point — Surfer-cottage remodels, outdoor showers, board storage, salt-spec finishes.
  • East Cliff Drive — Oceanfront homes with bluff-setback considerations and heavy weather exposure.
  • 17th Avenue & the Simpkins area — Post-war tract homes, first-time major remodels, kitchen and bath packages.
  • Live Oak Elementary / Brommer Street — Family homes with ADU or addition needs as kids get older.
  • Twin Lakes & Harbor edge — Boat-adjacent properties, storage-heavy garage remodels, ADUs used as rental income.

Coastal Commission timing

On any Live Oak project south of East Cliff, we submit the county permit and the CDP in parallel rather than sequentially. It's the single biggest schedule optimization we've found for Live Oak work.

Let's talk

Call (831) 234-4669 or use the form.

faq.q: Do I need a Coastal Development Permit for an interior remodel?
faq.a: Usually not. Interior-only work that doesn't change the building footprint or add bedrooms typically doesn't trigger a CDP. Exterior work, additions, and ADUs usually do.

name: Scotts Valley
h1: General Contractor in Scotts Valley, CA
metaTitle: Scotts Valley General Contractor | Remodels, ADUs & Additions | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Scotts Valley contractor for kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, additions, and whole-home renovations. Licensed CSLB #1075110. Free estimates.
lede: Scotts Valley is its own incorporated city with its own permit counter, its own design tastes, and a housing stock that ranges from 1970s planned developments to modern estate properties on the Glenwood ridge.
bodyHtml:
Scotts Valley homes are bigger, newer, and more forgiving

Most Scotts Valley remodels we do are second-phase improvements on homes built between 1975 and 2005 — updating dated oak cabinets, reconfiguring formal living-dining plans into open kitchen-family-room layouts, and adding primary suites. The framing is predictable, the electrical is to code, and the plumbing is copper and PEX. Surprises are fewer; aesthetics take the spotlight.

Scotts Valley has its own Building Department and its own design review. Permits are generally faster than unincorporated county work because the city's scale lets plan review stay responsive.

Areas we cover in Scotts Valley

  • Glenwood Drive & the ridge — Estate-scale remodels, outdoor living, view-preserving additions.
  • Graham Hill & Mount Hermon Road — Tract and semi-custom homes, workflow-focused kitchen remodels, primary suite additions.
  • El Pueblo & Kings Village — Older neighborhoods with a mix of additions, interior remodels, and ADU work.
  • Scotts Valley Drive corridor — 1990s–2000s homes, high-end kitchen remodels, accessory office builds.

Fire code matters here

Much of Scotts Valley falls within the Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI) zone. That means Chapter 7A of the California Building Code applies: ignition-resistant siding, ember-resistant vents, Class A roof assemblies, and non-combustible decking within 10 feet of the structure. We spec to WUI by default on any exterior work in Scotts Valley and won't let a client talk us out of it.

Start planning

Call (831) 234-4669. First conversation is free.

faq.q: What is a WUI zone and does it apply to me?
faq.a: Wildland-Urban Interface — areas where development meets wildfire-prone vegetation. Most of Scotts Valley, the San Lorenzo Valley, and parts of Aptos Hills are mapped WUI. If yours is, exterior materials must meet ignition-resistance standards. We'll check before quoting.

faq.q: Can you match the style of my 1990s Scotts Valley home?
faq.a: Yes — and we'll also show you respectful updates that modernize without turning a transitional home into a farmhouse caricature. Our work tends to go with the bones of the house.

name: Ben Lomond
h1: General Contractor in Ben Lomond, CA
metaTitle: Ben Lomond General Contractor | Mountain Home Remodels | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: San Lorenzo Valley contractor — Ben Lomond kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, additions, fire-rebuild-informed construction. CSLB #1075110.
lede: Ben Lomond and the rest of the San Lorenzo Valley are where we take on the county's most character-rich — and most challenging — remodels. Mountain homes, steep driveways, big redwoods, and post-2020-fire sensibilities.
bodyHtml:
Mountain construction is different

Ben Lomond work comes with access challenges you don't see down on the flats: one-lane driveways, 20% slopes, 80-foot redwoods we have to protect, and winter road closures that can shut down a site for a week. We scope all of that upfront so it doesn't turn into surprise change orders.

Ben Lomond is unincorporated Santa Cruz County. Much of it is designated WUI (Wildland-Urban Interface), which means exterior work has to meet Chapter 7A ignition-resistance standards. Many of our clients here are either fire-rebuilders or owners hardening their homes against the next fire.

Areas we cover in the SLV

  • Ben Lomond proper — Downtown cottages, creek-adjacent properties, character remodels.
  • Glen Arbor & Alba Road — Steeper lots, longer driveways, septic and well.
  • Brookdale & Boulder Creek edge — Classic mountain homes, fire-informed rebuilds and retrofits.
  • Felton — See the Felton page for details.

Fire-wise construction

We don't just meet WUI code — we go past it where the cost difference is small. Triple-pane windows, fully enclosed eaves, hardboard or metal siding, Class A roofs, non-combustible decks, and 100-foot defensible space planning. Insurance carriers are increasingly rewarding these specs with better rates, and the peace of mind during a red-flag day is worth it on its own.

Winter staging

We don't start foundation pours in the SLV in January or February unless there's no alternative — the rain risk is too high. For mountain clients we typically plan permit in fall, demo and foundation in late spring, and finish in fall of the following year.

Let's plan your mountain remodel

Call (831) 234-4669. We're comfortable on steep driveways and tight turnarounds.

faq.q: Do you take on fire-rebuild projects?
faq.a: Yes. We've worked with owners rebuilding after the CZU complex and can coordinate with your insurance adjuster, the county's rebuild-streamlining program, and the geotech reports those rebuilds require.

faq.q: Can you protect the redwoods on my lot?
faq.a: Absolutely. Critical Root Zone protection is part of our standard site-prep plan — we fence the drip line, require no-grade zones, and coordinate with a certified arborist when needed.

name: Felton
metaTitle: Felton General Contractor | SLV Remodels & ADUs | Gravity Construction
metaDescription: Felton remodel contractor — kitchen and bath remodels, ADUs, fire-rebuild-informed construction, WUI-rated exteriors. CSLB #1075110.
lede: Felton sits at the gateway to the San Lorenzo Valley — wooded, character-rich, and full of homes that deserve better than the minimum. We build and remodel throughout the 95018 zip, from Zayante to the edge of Henry Cowell.
bodyHtml:
Felton homes have soul and require patience

A typical Felton remodel we take on is a 1950s or 1960s cabin-style home that's been loved by three generations and now needs a kitchen that functions, a bathroom that's waterproof, and exterior hardening against fire. We understand how to preserve what makes these homes special — the tongue-and-groove ceilings, the stone fireplaces, the redwood paneling — while quietly upgrading everything that matters behind the walls.

Felton is unincorporated Santa Cruz County. Much of it is mapped as WUI. Permits come through County Planning; the rebuild-streamlining program set up after the CZU fires has significantly sped up post-fire work.

Areas we cover

  • Zayante & East Zayante — Cabin remodels, ADU builds, fire hardening.
  • Lompico — Steep driveways, creek-proximity, careful staging.
  • Mount Hermon Road & Graham Hill edge — Larger lots, semi-custom remodels and additions.
  • Henry Cowell / Roaring Camp edge — Riparian-adjacent properties with extra environmental review.

What Felton needs from a contractor

A willingness to drive. Comfort with narrow roads. A subcontractor bench that doesn't flinch when the address is on an unpaved lane. Rigor about fire code. Respect for old-growth trees. We check all of those boxes.

Call us

(831) 234-4669 — same team answers the phone as shows up on site.

faq.a: Most of it, yes. We'll verify your specific parcel before quoting, but plan on ignition-resistant exterior materials for any new construction or exterior replacement work.
